title: B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
spec: notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:10 › Cloned CHED-PP notification › B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification
outcome: Test FAILED at action 24: the clone search for phyto certificate PHYTO.FR.2026.0000064 (FR, issued 2026-04-15, party 'WorldUSIN74 Co') resolved to the cloning summary page, but that page returned HTTP 406 and rendered the blocked state 'You cannot clone this certificate' — so the 'Clone' button never existed and the click timed out after 30s. The notification was never cloned or submitted despite the test title.

Unable to clone (cloning summary).note: The certificate WAS found — this is a data-completeness block, not a not-found. IPAFFS offers a documented fallback path (download PDF + create a new notification with the cert attached) rather than a dead end.

findings: Clone-a-certificate is a distinct entry point into CHED-PP creation, separate from creating a notification from scratch: dashboard link 'Clone a certificate' -> choose CHED type -> search by certificate -> summary -> Clone.
The clone search key is a 4-part composite: country of origin + certificate reference + date of issue + consignor/consignee/importer name. All four appear required to resolve a single certificate.

The blocked state 'You cannot clone this certificate' is served with HTTP 406 Not Acceptable — an unusual status for a rendered GOV.UK error page (200 would be conventional). Worth NOT copying into the new app.

A cloneable certificate requires completeness: a certificate can exist and be retrievable (PDF downloadable) yet still be un-cloneable because it 'is missing some of the details needed to create a new notification'. The new app needs an explicit rule for which fields make a source record cloneable.

The fallback path is a requirement in its own right: when clone is blocked, offer (a) download the certificate PDF and (b) 'Create a new notification with attachment' — pre-attaching the certificate to accompanying documents.

COVERAGE GAP: this trace never reaches the actual clone or the submit. Despite the test title 'Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification', no clone, no pre-notification form, and no submission were exercised. The happy-path clone-then-submit journey is NOT evidenced by this trace and must be corroborated from another trace, the page objects (CloningCertificateDetailsPage.ts, ched-pp-workflows.ts:942-951) or templates.

Search criteria travel in the query string (ehcRef, origin, certificateDateOfIssue, partyName, certType), which leaks the trading party name into URLs, logs and the cookie-banner returnUrl. The snapshot shows partyName embedded in the Cookie Settings returnUrl — a PII-in-URL smell the new app should avoid.
